question.text: A Bermudan option is best described as:

answer.text: An option that can be exercised on specific dates before expiration
An option that can only be exercised at expiration
An option that can be exercised at any time before expiration
An option whose strike price changes on specific dates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Bermudan option allows exercise on specific pre-determined dates before expiry -- a hybrid between European (only at expiry) and American (any time). **The Trap:** 'Only at expiration' is European. 'Any time' is American. Changing strike prices describe other exotic options, not Bermudan. **Cross-Exam Context:** The three option styles (European, American, Bermudan) are tested in NISM Interest Rate and Equity Derivatives modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The name 'Bermudan' comes from Bermuda being geographically between America and Europe -- the option is a hybrid of American and European styles.
